faqs.question: How do I choose the right Rorebai solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Rorebai solution depends on your application requirements: (1) For communications systems - choose RF transceiver and high-speed data converter solutions; (2) For industrial measurement - choose precision ADC and op-amp solutions; (3) For sensor interfaces - choose precision op-amp and power management solutions; (4) For portable devices - choose low-power data converters and power management ICs. Consider factors like accuracy requirements, speed, power consumption, and environmental conditions. Contact LiTong FAE for personalized recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match solution to application requirements. Contact FAE for guidance.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for Rorebai solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Rorebai solutions: (1) Technical consultation - helping select the right components for your application; (2) Application engineering - supporting schematic design, PCB layout, and system integration; (3) Reference designs - providing proven design examples and evaluation boards; (4) Quality support - handling RMA processes and quality issues; (5) Design-in support - providing technical documentation and application notes; (6) Long-term supply - ensuring stable supply for production programs. Our FAE team has expertise in analog IC applications across industrial, communications, and medical markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: LiTong provides end-to-end support from design to production for Rorebai solutions.

faqs.question: Can Rorebai provide custom solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rorebai offers custom solutions for specific application requirements: (1) Custom specifications - tailored performance parameters for unique needs; (2) Special packages - non-standard form factors for space-constrained designs; (3) Extended temperature range - beyond standard specifications; (4) Special testing - additional screening or burn-in requirements; (5) Long-term supply - guaranteed availability for production programs. Custom solutions require minimum order quantities and development lead times. Contact LiTong to discuss your specific requirements and feasibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Custom solutions available for volume applications. Contact LiTong to discuss requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Rorebai solutions?
faqs.answer: Rorebai solution lead times vary by complexity: (1) Standard products - typically 4-6 weeks for common configurations; (2) Custom solutions - 8-12 weeks depending on specific requirements; (3) Volume production - 6-10 weeks for large orders. LiTong maintains strategic inventory for high-demand Rorebai products. For critical projects, we recommend forecasting and buffer stock arrangements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard solutions 4-6 weeks, custom solutions 8-12 weeks. Contact sales for specific lead times.

faqs.question: Does Rorebai offer evaluation kits for solution development?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rorebai offers evaluation kits for solution development: (1) Evaluation boards - complete hardware platform with all interfaces; (2) Software tools - configuration and data capture software; (3) Reference designs - proven hardware and software examples; (4) Technical documentation - detailed datasheets and application notes. Evaluation kits are available for qualified customers. Contact LiTong sales to request evaluation kits for your project.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluation kits available for qualified projects. Contact LiTong sales to request development kits.

solutions.name: Communication Receiver Solution
solutions.title: Rorebai Communication Receiver Solution
solutions.subtitle: High-performance RF and data conversion solution for wireless communication systems
solutions.description: Complete receiver solution featuring Rorebai RF transceivers and high-speed ADCs optimized for 4G/5G base stations and wireless infrastructure.
solutions.longDescription:
The Communication Receiver Solution from rorebai represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of rorebai components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
coreAdvantages: Wideband RF coverage for multi-band operation
High dynamic range for interference rejection
Low power consumption for thermal efficiency

faqs.question: What frequency bands does the solution support?
faqs.answer: The Communication Receiver Solution supports a wide frequency range from 300MHz to 6GHz, covering major cellular bands including: (1) Sub-1GHz bands - 450MHz, 700MHz, 800MHz, 900MHz for IoT and legacy systems; (2) 1-3GHz bands - 1.8GHz, 2.1GHz, 2.6GHz for 4G LTE; (3) 3-6GHz bands - 3.5GHz, 4.9GHz for 5G NR. The CBMRF9009 features dual independent RF PLLs enabling simultaneous reception of different bands. Contact LiTong FAE for specific band configuration support.

faqs.question: How do I optimize receiver sensitivity?
faqs.answer: Receiver sensitivity optimization involves several factors: (1) Noise figure - minimize front-end losses and use low-noise amplifiers; (2) ADC SNR - use clean clock source and proper input drive; (3) Bandwidth - match receiver bandwidth to signal bandwidth; (4) Interference rejection - use appropriate filtering. The solution achieves -110dBm sensitivity with 100kHz channel bandwidth. For best sensitivity, use external LNA with <1dB noise figure and ensure proper impedance matching. Contact LiTong FAE for sensitivity optimization guidance.

faeInsights.title: Senior FAE - RF Communications
faeInsights.summary: Proper receiver design requires careful attention to noise, linearity, and interference.
faeInsights.content: In my experience supporting communication receiver designs, the Rorebai solution provides excellent performance at a competitive price point. The key to achieving good sensitivity is minimizing noise figure in the front-end while maintaining adequate linearity for interference handling. I always recommend budgeting 0.5-1dB for implementation losses in the link budget. The CBMRF9009's dual PLL architecture is particularly useful for carrier aggregation applications. One common pitfall is neglecting clock jitter - use a clean clock source to achieve the ADC's full SNR specification. With proper design, this solution meets the requirements of most 4G/5G receiver applications.
faeInsights.insight: Receiver sensitivity is determined by the complete signal chain, not just the ADC.
faeInsights.logic: Each component in the signal chain contributes to overall noise figure and linearity.
keyTakeaways: Minimize front-end noise figure for best sensitivity
Use clean clock source for ADC

caseStudies.title: Industrial Sensor Interface
caseStudies.results: Achieved 0.01% accuracy over industrial temperature range

faqs.question: What accuracy can the solution achieve?
faqs.answer: The Precision Measurement Solution achieves excellent accuracy: (1) ADC linearity - ±1 LSB INL and ±0.5 LSB DNL; (2) Offset error - <5µV with chopper-stabilized op-amps; (3) Gain error - <0.01% with precision reference; (4) Noise - 8nV/√Hz enabling microvolt-level resolution; (5) Drift - <0.05ppm/°C with proper design. Overall system accuracy of 0.01% is achievable with careful PCB layout and calibration. Contact LiTong FAE for accuracy optimization guidance.

faqs.question: How do I minimize noise in precision measurements?
faqs.answer: Noise minimization techniques: (1) Shielding - use shielded cables and enclosures; (2) Filtering - implement appropriate analog and digital filtering; (3) Grounding - use star grounding and minimize ground loops; (4) Layout - keep analog traces short and away from digital signals; (5) Power - use clean power supplies with adequate decoupling. The solution achieves 8nV/√Hz noise density. For best results, implement anti-aliasing filter with cutoff at 0.5x sampling rate. Contact LiTong FAE for noise analysis support.

faeInsights.title: Principal FAE - Precision Analog
faeInsights.summary: Precision measurement requires attention to noise, drift, and linearity.
faeInsights.content: Precision measurement design is all about managing error sources. In my experience, thermal EMF in PCB traces can cause microvolt-level errors that limit system accuracy. I always recommend using low-thermal EMF solder and keeping temperature gradients minimal. The CBM92AD16's ±1 LSB INL is excellent, but you need an equally good reference voltage to realize that accuracy. Use the CBMREF16 or a precision external reference. For sensor interfaces, the CBMOPA216's 5µV offset enables direct measurement of small signals. Remember that accuracy is determined by the weakest link in the signal chain.
faeInsights.insight: System accuracy is limited by the worst error source in the signal chain.
faeInsights.logic: All error sources - offset, gain, linearity, noise - contribute to total measurement uncertainty.

solutions.name: Sensor Interface Solution
solutions.title: Rorebai Sensor Interface Solution
solutions.subtitle: Complete signal conditioning solution for industrial and IoT sensor applications
solutions.description: Integrated sensor interface solution featuring Rorebai precision op-amps, ADCs, and power management for temperature, pressure, and flow sensor applications.
solutions.longDescription:
The Sensor Interface Solution from Rorebai provides a complete signal conditioning platform for industrial and IoT sensor applications. This solution integrates precision analog front-end components with efficient power management to deliver accurate, reliable sensor measurements in demanding environments.

The solution supports a wide variety of sensor types including RTDs, thermocouples, strain gauges, and pressure sensors. Programmable gain amplifiers enable interfacing with sensors having output ranges from microvolts to volts. Built-in excitation sources and ratiometric measurement techniques ensure accurate, drift-resistant readings.

Key features include chopper-stabilized amplifiers for microvolt-level offset, 24-bit ADC resolution for precise measurement, and isolated power supply options for industrial environments. The solution achieves excellent accuracy over the full industrial temperature range of -40°C to +85°C.

Implementation is supported by comprehensive reference designs for common sensor types, including RTD measurement circuits, thermocouple interfaces with cold junction compensation, and 4-20mA loop-powered transmitters. LiTong's FAE team provides expert guidance on sensor selection, signal conditioning design, and calibration procedures.

The solution has been deployed in industrial process control, building automation, environmental monitoring, and smart agriculture applications with proven reliability and performance.
coreAdvantages: Universal sensor compatibility with programmable gain
Microvolt-level offset for small signal sensors
Isolated power options for industrial use

customerCases.solution: Used CBM24AD01 with sleep mode and ratiometric measurement

faqs.question: What sensor types are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The Sensor Interface Solution supports multiple sensor types: (1) Temperature - RTDs (Pt100, Pt1000), thermocouples (J, K, T, E, N, S, R, B types); (2) Pressure - strain gauge, piezoresistive, capacitive; (3) Flow - differential pressure, turbine, magnetic; (4) Level - capacitive, ultrasonic, radar; (5) General - 4-20mA loops, 0-10V signals, bridge sensors. The programmable gain amplifier accommodates sensor outputs from ±10mV to ±10V. Contact LiTong FAE for specific sensor interface design support.

faqs.question: How do I achieve high accuracy with thermocouples?
faqs.answer: Thermocouple accuracy optimization: (1) Cold junction compensation - use accurate temperature sensor at connection point; (2) Reference junction - CBMREF20 provides stable 2.5V reference; (3) Amplifier - CBMOPA201's <1µV offset preserves small TC signals; (4) Filtering - implement 10Hz low-pass for noise reduction; (5) Linearization - use built-in ADC lookup tables or polynomial correction. The solution achieves ±0.5°C accuracy for K-type thermocouples over -200°C to +1372°C range. Contact LiTong FAE for thermocouple design guidance.

faqs.question: What isolation options are available?
faqs.answer: Isolation options for the Sensor Interface Solution: (1) Power isolation - CBMPWR10 provides 2500Vrms isolated DC-DC; (2) Signal isolation - external isolation amplifiers available; (3) Digital isolation - isolated SPI interface using digital isolators; (4) Channel-to-channel - individual isolation for multi-channel systems; (5) Protection - isolated designs withstand industrial transients. Isolation is recommended for industrial environments with high EMI or ground potential differences. Contact LiTong FAE for isolated design recommendations.

faeInsights.title: Principal FAE - Sensor Interface
faeInsights.summary: Sensor interface design requires balancing accuracy, noise, and power constraints.
faeInsights.content: I've designed sensor interfaces for countless industrial applications, and the key is understanding your error budget. The CBM24AD01's 24-bit resolution is meaningless if your front-end adds 100µV of offset. That's why I always recommend chopper-stabilized amplifiers like the CBMOPA201 for sensor applications. For thermocouples, cold junction compensation is critical - use a good local temperature sensor and keep the connection point isothermal. For RTDs, 3-wire or 4-wire configurations eliminate lead resistance errors. Remember that ratiometric measurement eliminates reference drift. With proper design, this solution achieves accuracy that rivals much more expensive dedicated sensor transmitters.
faeInsights.insight: Sensor accuracy is determined by the complete signal chain from sensor to digital output.
faeInsights.logic: Each element - sensor, wiring, amplification, conversion - contributes to total measurement uncertainty.
keyTakeaways: Use chopper-stabilized amplifiers for low offset
bestPractices: Keep analog traces away from digital
